channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: DOGE isn’t Silicon Valley innovation—it’s just a sloppy rebrand of free-market dogma
item.description: At a press conference in the Oval Office earlier this month, Elon Musk—a billionaire who is not, at least formally, the President of the United States—was asked how the Department of Government Efficiency manages potential conflicts of interest to ensure “accountability and transparency.
item.dc:creator: Jay Willis, Fast Company

item.title: Apple unveils 'age assurance' technology amid child safety push
item.description: Apple will introduce "age assurance" technology to boost children's privacy and safety on the company's devices, the company announced Thursday as part of its broader push to protect kids online.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: What is Apple Intelligence: everything you need to know about the AI toolkit
item.description: Apple Intelligence is a built-in AI toolkit for iPhone, iPad and Mac users. It’s designed to make life easier through suggestions, automations and personalization. Here’s why it’s so useful.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: What is an AI server? Why artificial intelligence needs specialized systems
item.description: AI servers are advanced computing systems designed to handle complex, resource-intensive AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft’s latest bit of nagging in Windows 11 might come from a good place, but it’s seriously annoying some people
item.description: New piece of stealth advertising in Windows 11 is a warning to back up your PC – and while that’s good advice, not everyone needs prodding to use OneDrive.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Sora Launches in the UK, EU, and Other European Countries: What You Need to Know
item.description: OpenAI's Sora launches in the UK, EU, and other European countries, but you need a ChatGPT Plus or Pro account to use it. Video quality and length depend on how much you pay.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The iPhone 16e doesn’t have MagSafe, but apparently Apple thinks you didn’t want it anyway
item.description: The iPhone 16e is finally out, but compared to the iPhone 14 it replaces there are a few missing key features – though when it comes to MagSafe, Apple seems to think you didn’t want it in the first place.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Temu is cheaper than archrival Amazon by 40% on average - but not the most popular products, research finds
item.description: Aggressive discounting helps Temu compete, yet ongoing trust concerns may limit its ability to overtake Amazon.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: IBM closes Hashicorp buy, looks to boost enterprise multicloud and AI automation technology
item.description: In announcing the closure of its $6.4 billion buy of Hashicorp, IBM said its goal is to infuse HashiCorp automation and security technology in every data center possible.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bipartisan senators unveil bill banning DeepSeek on government devices
item.description: A bipartisan group of senators introduced legislation Thursday to ban Chinese artificial intelligence (AI) startup DeepSeek’s products from government devices and networks. The bill, introduced by Sens. Jacky Rosen (D-Nev.), Jon Husted (R-Ohio) and Pete Ricketts (R-Neb.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Gabbard decries Britain's reported demand for Apple to provide backdoor access to users' cloud data
item.description: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard says she has serious concerns about the British government’s reported demand that Apple provide backdoor access to any data stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: One Tech Tip: Getting a lot of unwanted phone calls? Here are ways to stop them
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it's a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it's enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Vietnam plans energy shift toward building more solar, less reliance on gas and coal
item.description: Vietnam is revising its energy plans to focus more on large solar farms and less on reliance on coal and natural gas.
item.dc:creator: Aniruddha Ghosal, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Nvidia sales surge in the fourth quarter on demand for AI chips
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia on Wednesday reported a surge in fourth-quarter profit and sales as demand for its specialized Blackwell chips, which power artificial intelligence systems, continued to grow, sending the company's stock higher after hours. For the three months that ended Jan.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Man who said he was upset with Musk and Trump charged with threatening to burn down xAI facility
item.description: ASHLAND CITY, Tenn. (AP) — A Tennessee man has been charged with an act of terrorism after he threatened to burn down an xAI facility because he was upset with its founder, Elon Musk, and President Donald Trump, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alibaba open sources its video-generation AI model
item.description: Chinese cloud provider Alibaba has released four versions of its video-generation AI model as open source, allowing users to download and run them for free on capable PCs.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Former Navy comptroller shares how financial management can successfully impact decision-making
item.description: Modern financial systems offer the ideal way to tie budget to strategy and improve decision-making, explains Russ Rumbaugh, who left the Navy in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: As Microsoft innovates, Apple stagnates
item.description: Microsoft and Apple were once the yin and yang of the tech world. Microsoft was dull and plodding, milking buggy, barely-good-enough-for-prime-time Windows for tremendous profits, thanks to a worldwide operating system monopoly.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Top Apple exec concedes he had doubts about app store fees imposed on alternative payment options
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Longtime Apple executive Phil Schiller on Monday conceded he initially opposed fees that the iPhone maker charges for in app-transactions processed outside its own payment system because he feared violating a court-ordered mandate designed to create more competition.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Friday ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, NewsNation
